CD36-positive tumors refer to malignant neoplasms that overexpress the CD36 transmembrane glycoprotein, a key molecule involved in cellular processes including lipid metabolism, inflammation, tumor initiation, progression, and metastasis. The overexpression of CD36 enhances the metastatic potential and disease progression of various cancers, serving as a biomarker for high-grade malignancies and a negative prognostic indicator.
